cases.prompt: $achieve make the accumulated local commits safe to push and give me a confidence report for what changed, what was verified, and what stays risky.
cases.repo_shape: repo with a long-running autonomous objective worth shaping into a reviewable goal before activation
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `achieve` instead of an adjacent public skill
does not invent host-specific adapter state when repo inspection alone should be enough
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
saves a draft goal artifact under `charness-artifacts/goals/<yyyy-mm-dd-slug>.md` with an explicit `/goal @...` activation line
tells the user the file is inert until activation and does not start executing slices itself
observed_evidence: Static review only: the checked-in core, references, helper scripts, validator suite, and two fresh-eye subagent passes were inspected. `achieve` was not executed end-to-end because `/goal` activation is host-driven and was not run, so no live-run proof is claimed.

Issue #223 (2026-05-27): the duplicate-test-pressure contract additions (Before-phase expected proof cost / test-duplication pressure, the During-phase cheap `--test-pressure` slice sample carried forward across compaction, and the After-phase new-slice-local vs accumulated-suite-debt classification) were reviewed by a 3-angle + counterweight fresh-eye critique. The new `Test duplication pressure` slice field is a backward-compatible additive label (no REQUIRED_SECTIONS change) guarded by `test_render_slice_block_includes_test_duplication_pressure`.

The checked-in `achieve` core Guardrails forbid generic task-runner behavior, auto-start before activation, handoff-as-running-scratchpad, broad gates after every small commit, and claiming live/provider proof when only local checks ran, which matches the design non-goals.

The goal artifact is created at `Status: draft` with an explicit `/goal @...` activation line, and `upsert_goal.py` never overwrites an existing body (it only rewrites the `Status:` line and only when it changes), so a reviewable activation artifact is produced rather than a runaway goal.

Metrics are represented honestly as host-agnostic shallow signals via `probe_host_logs.py` with deep Codex audit best-effort and `Metrics: when available`, instead of fabricating token or tool-call totals the host log does not expose.

The cross-skill behavior is owned by a presence-gated shared reference (`skills/shared/references/active-goal-coordination.md`) cited by impl and issue, with quality/critique cadence coordinated by achieve through the same reference; it is a silent no-op when no active goal artifact exists, so each coordinated skill stays useful standalone.

Two fresh-eye subagent passes found no correctness blockers in the four `achieve` scripts or the goal artifact helpers; slice append produces no trailing whitespace or doubled blank lines and `check_goal_artifact.py` passes on a scaffolded artifact.

cases.prompt: Summarize the latest repo changes into a chat-ready update and keep the draft scoped to what changed.
cases.repo_shape: repo with recent checked-in changes and one clear delivery context such as release notes or a team update
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `announcement` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/announcement/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps `announcement` draft-first, explicitly separating human-facing communication from delivery backends and from `narrative` source-of-truth alignment work.

The adapter resolver already pins the visible draft artifact to `charness-artifacts/announcement/latest.md` and the hidden delivery record to `.charness/announcement/announcements.jsonl`, with draft-only defaults when no delivery config exists.

Repo-owned tests already cover both seams: `test_announcement_resolve_adapter_explains_draft_only_defaults` guards the draft-only fallback and `test_capability_explain_reports_skill_needs_and_announcement_adapter_binding` proves adapter-defined delivery capability binding.

cases.prompt: We keep adding ad hoc scripts here; normalize this into one repo-owned CLI before the command surface sprawls further.
cases.repo_shape: tooling repo where ad hoc shell or Python entrypoints already exist and the command surface needs to be normalized
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `create-cli` instead of an adjacent public skill
does not invent host-specific adapter state when repo inspection alone should be enough
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core frames `create-cli` as command-surface normalization before ad hoc entrypoints sprawl, keeping operator contract, lifecycle commands, and machine-readable output ahead of implementation detail.

The current validation policy already classifies `create-cli` as `hitl-recommended` and `adapter-free`, which matches a repo-inspection-first planning skill instead of a durable-artifact or adapter-owned workflow.

Repo-owned tests already pin the most load-bearing seam here: `test_quality_and_create_cli_carry_command_docs_drift_pattern` and `test_create_cli_and_create_skill_carry_authenticated_release_probe_pattern` preserve the command-doc drift and authenticated release-probe contracts.

cases.prompt: Improve this skill package first so the trigger, references, and helper surface stay portable.
cases.repo_shape: skills repo with adjacent public/support surfaces, references, and packaging constraints already present
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `create-skill` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/create-skill` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
treats the public skill frontmatter and core trigger as classifier input, not only documentation
keeps `SKILL.md` as selection/sequence core and pushes bulky nuance into references or scripts
starts public-skill adapter/bootstrap/example changes from the changed skill's customer journey
observed_evidence: The prompt still routes directly to `create-skill`, and the checked-in core keeps public-skill editing anchored on contract freezing, customer-of-this-skill critique, portable helper extraction, and classifier-aware `SKILL.md` edits rather than generic implementation drift.
The checked-in core now resolves the create-skill adapter so repo-local implementation topology terms stay outside portable skill prose while missing adapters remain visible.

The checked-in core now requires scheduled, async, and external-lookup skill self-tests to replay the commands, inputs, selectors, and adapter state prescribed by `SKILL.md`, with raw provider response evidence before choosing a stable identification signal.

The adapter-pattern reference now says visible artifacts are history-default and `latest.md` is only an optional current pointer, which is the intended portable authoring rule for future skill packages instead of a one-off repo exception.

The current `cautilus` proof policy around `create-skill` no longer treats every scenario-review-worthy prompt change as ask-before-run: adaptive mode now auto-runs proof and reserves confirmation for maintained scenario-registry mutation.

The portable-authoring Verification step now backs the ownership-overlap rule with a runtime validator: `scripts/check_skill_ownership_overlap.py` flags any cross-namespace mention of `charness-artifacts/<other>/` or `.agents/<other>-adapter.yaml` and requires an explicit allowlist entry with a written reason, so the boundary is no longer prose-only enforcement.

cases.prompt: Investigate this regression and leave a durable record of what actually failed before changing code.
cases.repo_shape: active repo slice with a reproducible failure, existing logs or tests, and enough local state to preserve a durable debug artifact
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `debug` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/debug/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core explicitly keeps `debug` in diagnosis-before-repair mode, preserving the exact symptom, a falsifiable hypothesis, and at least three candidate causes before implementation work starts.
For async, scheduled, or user-visible workflows, the debug core now separates pre-worker acknowledgement, worker execution, and post-worker side effects before reasoning only about worker behavior.

The debug core now keeps critique out of premature fact-gathering while requiring task-completing debug closeout or repair handoff to record short scoped diagnosis/repair risk or full standalone critique for broader seams.
Repo-owned eval coverage already exercises `debug-adapter-bootstrap`, which pins the current adapter bootstrap and canonical artifact path at `charness-artifacts/debug/latest.md`.

The committed `validate_debug_artifact.py` gate plus `tests/test_debug_artifact.py` now make the current debug artifact carry structured seam risk and interruption data, so a host-boundary miss cannot stay a chat-only handoff.
`tests/test_debug_scaffold.py` now proves the scaffold-emitted validator command works from an exported plugin layout in a clean consumer repo.

The scaffold payload now exposes `write_artifact_path` and resolves a symlinked `latest.md` to the real current-pointer target, so consumer agents do not need to discover the symlink by accidental hand edits.

`debug` now owns the full bug-class RCA substrate: step 6 invokes `references/detection-gap.md` and `references/sibling-search.md` alongside `references/five-whys-causal-chain.md`, and the validator requires `## Detection Gap` and `## Sibling Search` sections so standalone debug carries the same patterns `issue resolve` bug-class consumes cite-only.

The sibling-search substrate now requires each structural sibling to keep the decision (`same bug, fix now`, diagnostic-only, intentional boundary, or deferred follow-up) separate from proof level (`static scan only`, local payload proof, provider roundtrip, or not inspected), so bug fixes cannot stop at the first symptom without a reviewer-visible classification ledger.

cases.prompt: Which skill should handle this named capability, and what existing support surface already covers it?
cases.repo_shape: repo with multiple public and support skills where the user names a capability instead of a file path
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `find-skills` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/find-skills/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
uses the named skill or capability lookup path before falling back to broad filesystem search
in `--read-only` mode skips the durable artifact write and reports `artifacts.mode = "read-only"` instead of refreshing `charness-artifacts/find-skills/latest.*`
observed_evidence: Integration entries in `charness-artifacts/find-skills/latest.json` now carry the real on-disk manifest path (`integrations/tools/<file>.json`) and the manifest's verbatim `intent_triggers`/`supports_public_skills`/`recommendation_role` values, while the `id` field continues to use the public-facing alias from `PROVIDER_ID_ALIASES`. This keeps `validate_current_pointer_freshness` able to pin manifest-to-inventory equality (path lookup and validator-checked field comparison) without breaking the user-facing provider abstraction.

The checked-in public core now treats `charness-artifacts/find-skills/latest.*` as the canonical capability inventory artifact, while recommendation queries stay ad hoc and should not rewrite that durable surface just because the invocation shape changed.

The current repo memory at `charness-artifacts/retro/recent-lessons.md` explicitly records the repeat trap here: canonical inventory and ad hoc recommendation output must stay separate so ordinary inspection does not create false dirty state or erode operator trust.

That keeps `find-skills-canonical-artifact-followup` as a reviewed consumer seam worth re-promoting after the latest chatbot packet churn: the user's push to distinguish canonical evidence from query-shaped output still maps directly to checked-in `find-skills` behavior and artifact policy.

`find-skills --recommend-for-task` now covers support-skill discoverability when workflow language implies a hidden support surface; the Specdown fixture routes `.spec.md`, `docs/specs`, and `check:jq` to `support/specdown` without polluting the canonical inventory artifact.

The canonical inventory artifact now has a durable `workflow_integrations` section for worktree create/cleanup while keeping ad hoc `workflow_recommendations` query output out of the persisted startup artifact.
Worktree workflow recommendations now point at packaged `integrations/worktree/adapter.example.yaml` instead of source-only docs, so installed plugin users can read the emitted path.

The task-text recommendation path is bounded by negative proof: weak singleton or generic `report.json` / doctest language without a strong Specdown anchor does not route unrelated work through `support/specdown`.

Slack URL task text now surfaces `gather-slack` from support capability intent triggers in read-only recommendation mode, so `find-skills` no longer hides the checked-in Slack gather wrapper behind a broad no-match note.

cases.prompt: Fetch this external source into a durable local artifact instead of giving me a one-turn summary.
cases.repo_shape: repo that already keeps gathered artifacts and a source identity that may need refresh-in-place behavior
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `gather` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/gather/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core now carries the private SaaS decision ladder explicitly: prefer existing local assets and official API/export paths first, fall back to `agent-browser` only when the stronger path is unavailable or equally gated, and stop cleanly when auth/bootstrap is still missing.

The prompt-affecting preserve proof at `charness-artifacts/cautilus/latest.md` stayed green while that contract widened, which is the current repo-owned evidence that the maintained first-skill routing surface did not regress when browser-mediated acquisition language was added.

The long-context proposal and benchmark surfaces still both carry `gather-official-path-before-browser-followup`, and the checked-in chatbot summaries now preserve the full `proposals` truth surface alongside the bounded `attentionView` shortlist instead of relying on a packet-level `limit` workaround. That keeps the `official API/export -> browser fallback -> clean auth/bootstrap stop` correction visible as a live consumer-facing gather seam instead of a stale side packet.

`gather_public_url.py` now proves the arbitrary-public-URL seam without live network dependence: the focused test writes a dated gather record containing the `support/web-fetch` route, acquisition trace, selected attempt, disposition, final status/confidence, open gaps, and embedded trace JSON.

The public capability contract now says arbitrary public URL gather must preserve the `support/web-fetch` acquisition trace, including attempted, skipped, unavailable, terminal, selected, and proof/confidence fields, so the standing gather/web-fetch invariant no longer lives only in `docs/handoff.md`.

The live public-URL dogfood record `charness-artifacts/gather/2026-05-16-rfc-editor-org-rfc-rfc9110-html-b1b13a12.md` proves `defuddle-reader-extraction` can be selected with strong proof against a real public URL, and the artifact now avoids listing `prior-stage-sufficient` skipped stages as open gaps.

`advise_slack_path.py` now resolves `gather_provider.slack.mode` and points direct-cli runs at `support/gather-slack/scripts/export-thread.sh` plus the runtime contract before browser-mediated private-source fallbacks.

`gather_public_url.py --persist-extracted-content` now stores opt-in readable extracted text/markdown for the selected successful attempt while keeping raw response bodies out of the default artifact and out of the trace JSON; JSON/API responses with positive proof report persistence as `unavailable` instead of writing raw API bodies.

The public core preserves exact adapter vocabulary for provider selection: examples refer to `gather_provider.<source>.mode`, and closeout names `access mode` so operator records do not blur schema keys into generic route wording.

The maintained Cautilus registry still maps `gather` to `gather-adapter-bootstrap`; this slice does not add a scenario because the closed risk is schema-vocabulary drift in the public core, covered by checked-in dogfood evidence and deterministic skill validation.

cases.prompt: Normalize this partially initialized repo without pretending it needs a greenfield rewrite.
cases.repo_shape: partially initialized mature repo with divergent but valid naming and intentionally missing optional surfaces
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `setup` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/setup/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps repo-mode detection explicit while adding a task-completing delegated-review gate for normalization runs; inspect/help/routing calls stay cheap.

`inspect_repo.py` now emits `recommendations[]` separately from `normalization.findings`, so adapter-declared policy sources can raise `agents.delegated_review_policy` without pretending phrase matching is a hard fact.

`inspect_repo.py` now flags `fresh_eye_task_review_scope_drift` when `AGENTS.md` carries a critique or fresh-eye delegation rule but does not name task-completing `setup` and `quality` review runs as spawn-authorized scopes.

`inspect_repo.py` keeps explicit repo inspection resilient by bounding source-guard discovery to AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/, and specs/ by default; repos that keep guards elsewhere can set `source_guard_scan_roots`, and unreadable specs become structured warnings instead of aborting install/update recovery.

The generated compact Skill Routing block now tells agents to run `find-skills --recommend-for-task "<task>"` when a request names workflow or capability nouns such as worktree, browser automation, specdown, or validation before ad hoc shell/tool use.

The setup core now keeps optional bootstrap seam details in `references/bootstrap-seams.md`; usage episodes remain setup-seeded, quality-validated, and runtime-owned by the product plus `usage-episodes` integration.

cases.prompt: File or resolve this GitHub issue through `gh`, keeping issue identity and freshness grounded in GitHub state.
cases.repo_shape: repo connected to GitHub where issue creation or issue resolution should use `gh` and GitHub state as the source of truth
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `issue` instead of an adjacent public skill
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
uses GitHub as the source of truth for omitted issue selection instead of session memory
keeps `issue new` problem-first and solution direction weak unless the user already fixed the implementation contract
uses the `github-gh` integration path for GitHub mutations instead of inventing a separate provider
emits a pre-mutation resolution brief for `feature` and `deferred-work` issues and pauses for user discussion when `open decisions` is non-empty
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core explicitly fixes GitHub as the source of truth for issue identity and freshness, including the omitted-selector rule that queries newest open GitHub issue state instead of the current session.
`issue new` keeps the receiving repo's problem context first and only allows weak candidate solution direction, preserving the cross-repo issue hygiene already used by narrative.

`issue_tool.py` gives the first-use path structured helpers for target resolution, `gh` preflight, and issue selector parsing, with tests covering default `corca-ai` ownership, current-repo inference, numeric ranges, and the GitHub-SoT wording.

`issue_close.close_with_comment` now enforces a per-op placeholder allowlist at runtime: `comment` accepts `{repo,number,body_file,reason}` and `close` accepts `{repo,number,reason}`. An adapter template using an unknown placeholder fails fast with the offending placeholder named, so the documented contract no longer relies on test-only enforcement.

Step 6 of the resolve flow now requires a pre-mutation resolution brief for `feature` and `deferred-work` issues. The adapter exposes `feature_brief_pause` (`on-open-decisions` default, `always`, `never`); invocation flags `--auto` and `--pause` override per call. Non-empty `open decisions` always force a pause. `issue_tool.py brief-path` resolves the persistent artifact under `charness-artifacts/issue/`, and `audit_brief.py` plus the bundled transcript fixtures verify that mutation events never precede brief events for feature-class fix-units.

Bug-class causal review now triages the `debug` substrate cite-only across all three lenses: Lens 1 cites `debug/references/five-whys-causal-chain.md`, Lens 2 cites `debug/references/detection-gap.md`, and Lens 3 cites `debug/references/sibling-search.md`. The subagent contract requires the substrate cite plus the `do not re-derive` guard verbatim for each lens, so `issue` no longer holds a private detection-gap or sibling-search body that could drift from the `debug` substrate.

Release-bundled issue resolution now has an explicit closeout bridge: `issue` tells operators to pass `--close-issue <number>` to the release helper and require `issue_closeout.status: verified`, while the closeout discipline reference preserves GitHub auto-close as the preferred carrier and manual close as the post-verification fallback.
`issue_tool.py close-with-comment` now re-reads GitHub issue state after comment plus close and fails if the issue is still open, so command success alone is not treated as source-of-truth closeout.

Bug-class close comments now preserve sibling decisions and proof levels from the debug sibling-search substrate, including diagnostic-only and intentional-boundary findings when they matter for recurrence risk.

`issue_tool.py verify-closeout` now distinguishes `carrier_verified` from final `status: verified`: direct commit and PR-body carriers must include GitHub close keywords plus classification ledger fields, manual fallback requires a structured fallback reason, and final handoff uses the selected backend's `view` operation with `--expect-state CLOSED`.

Scenario review for this semantic change kept the maintained Cautilus issue fixtures unchanged: `issue-146-sibling-search` and `issue-148-sibling-search` still cover causal-review/sibling-search routing, while the new closeout verifier is covered by deterministic command tests in `tests/quality_gates/test_issue_closeout_verifier.py`.

cases.prompt: Tighten the repo's durable story first, then derive one concise brief from that source of truth.
cases.repo_shape: repo with existing source-of-truth docs that drift from the current product or project story
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `narrative` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/narrative/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps durable truth alignment separate from audience adaptation and explicitly hands delivery-only work to `announcement`.
The adapter resolves the durable artifact to `charness-artifacts/narrative/latest.md`, which matches the reviewed expectation for the aligned truth-surface output.

Repo-owned tests and docs already exercise the narrative adapter, source-map path, and narrative versus announcement boundary, which is enough local evidence to treat first-touch story alignment as a real maintained seam.

cases.prompt: Stress this pending decision before we lock it in and separate real blockers from over-worry.
cases.repo_shape: repo with a non-trivial pending decision whose main risk is choosing the wrong plan too early
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `critique` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/critique` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
includes a customer-of-this-capability angle when first-use failure is the main risk
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core now says task-completing repo work always records critique before closeout, scaling the pass rather than leaving applicability optional.

The standalone `critique` skill remains the full bounded-subagent path for non-trivial design, deletion, rename, release, workflow, compatibility, install/update, host-proof, prompt-surface, public-skill, validator, or export decisions.
The angle-selection reference now names `customer-of-this-capability` so capability changes can test the user's or downstream agent's first real use instead of only producer-side breakage.

That same contract now makes blocked execution explicit in the public output shape, so a host-side subagent gap stays visible as `Execution: blocked <host-signal>` instead of being rewritten as a degraded concern list.
That keeps `critique-canonical-subagent-followup` live as a reviewed seam: the repo now locks the user push for true subagent critique in both checked-in wording and validator-backed contract pins.
The capability check now rejects shell-only runners, routing-only proof, and model self-report as evidence that bounded subagents actually ran.

The capability check now treats an `AGENTS.md` `Subagent Delegation` section as the explicit delegation request for named bounded review scopes, so downstream repos such as `ceal` should try the host spawn tool before recording a blocked critique.
`validate_critique_artifacts.py` now rejects new durable critique artifacts that preserve the old missing-explicit-subagent-request blocker instead of a concrete `host signal:` or `tool signal:`.

The `critique` skill now reads `.agents/critique-adapter.yaml` and consumes a `charness.critique_prepare_packet.v1` packet under `charness-artifacts/critique/` before fresh-eye review when the adapter declares `packet_sections`, with a `Packet Consumed` field in the output shape (#161).

The committed-diff critique dogfood on `50dd2b7` found that a clean working tree can erase packet changed-surface context; `prepare_packet.py --changed-ref HEAD^..HEAD` now preserves the reviewed commit/range for script packet sections without requiring a maintained Cautilus scenario.

`prepare_packet.py --commit <sha>` and `--range <base>..<head>` now alias the explicit changed-ref path, store `changed_ref`, and make changed-surface packet output name the reviewed ref/range instead of implying a working-tree critique.

cases.prompt: Review why the standing test gate feels slow, including local vs CI runtime differences, and install the next deterministic gate if the move is obvious.
cases.repo_shape: mature repo with standing local gates, runtime budgets, possible local-vs-CI machine variance, and at least one final stop-before-finish command
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `quality` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/quality/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
runs or names the existing repo-owned quality gates before proposing new ones
attempts bounded subagent review or records a concrete blocked host/tool signal before broad slow-gate recommendations
checks fixture economics, parallel critical path, duplicated proof, and runtime profile policy instead of only widening budgets
treats affected-test selection as structural testability before recommending caches, observation tools, or broader runtime budgets
uses one realistic consumer prompt when the risk is public-skill routing or artifact behavior
observed_evidence: Issue #223 (2026-05-27): the testability reference now distinguishes structural test cleanup (extract fixture builders, lifecycle wrappers, command runners, shared assertions while keeping behavioral intent in `.test.*`) from hiding test bodies in support files, and requires naming the smallest next structural cleanup on broad duplicate/pressure-gate failure instead of only reporting the percentage. A 3-angle + counterweight fresh-eye critique confirmed the boundary stays consumer-owned: the duplicate/length gate remains a repo-local deterministic gate, not a Charness-owned runner, consistent with `references/behavior-testing.md`.

The current quality posture still treats checked-in proof artifacts, public executable readers, and lower deterministic gates as separate layers with different ownership, which keeps `quality` distinct from generic test-running or release work.

Maintainer-local enforcement now asks whether local or pre-push gates hide network, external-repo fetch, latest-release, or supply-chain refresh work that should be an explicit refresh/update/release action instead of default local proof.

The prompt-asset reference now makes the adaptive `cautilus` split explicit: scenario review can run automatically as proof, while maintained scenario-registry mutation remains the higher-friction follow-up that still needs confirmation.
That preserves the consumer-facing `quality` contract here: the skill should prefer repo-owned proof and enforcement structure over ad hoc ceremony, including for prompt-surface validation policy.

The missing-validation-binary rule keeps the same contract for local tools: when an existing gate cannot run because `ruff` or another manifest-backed binary is absent, `quality` should turn that into setup work with install and verify steps instead of reporting a skipped gate.

The quality core now requires task-completing reviews to report delegated-review status and includes an adapter/gate design inventory path for recommendation, acknowledgement, migration, and brittle hard-gate risks.

The quality core and standing-gate verbosity reference now require slow-test reviews to prove the active parallel-runner path, inspect serial fallback, run duration reports, and surface top-N runtime hot spots before widening budgets or moving checks on-demand.

The quality core now requires task-completing delegated review after initial inventory and before broad recommendations, and slow-gate advice must name or block fixture-economics, parallel-critical-path, and duplicated-proof review lenses.

The quality core now runs `inventory_standing_test_economics.py` when standing-test economics are in scope, so slow-gate review checks test-file count, runner isolation/process startup, transpiler-loader cost, nested CLI fanout, and duplicated proof before recommending test pruning.
The slow-gate reference now preserves the Ceal dogfood lesson that `test files * runner isolation * loader startup` can dominate wall time even when individual subtests look cheap.
`validate_quality_artifact.py` now requires `Advisory` and `Delegated Review` sections, rejecting green-gate-only summaries that omit advisory or delegated-review status.
The quality adapter resolver now preserves existing `version: 1` adapters while defaulting new review fields to safe empty values.

`inventory_entrypoint_docs_ergonomics.py` now surfaces `host_instruction_runbook_pressure` when `AGENTS.md` embeds a multi-step procedure that likely belongs in a deeper doc or repo-owned command surface.

`inventory_brittle_source_guards.py` keeps advisory source-guard review resilient by bounding default scan roots, exposing explicit `--scan-root` overrides, and reporting unreadable specs without crashing the quality run.
The quality core now makes domain-language alignment explicit: user-facing wording, CLI/config names, code names, and artifact names should converge on adapter-declared canonical terms.
`inventory_ubiquitous_language.py` dogfoods that contract as an advisory inventory that becomes a hard failure only for low-noise deprecated aliases declared in `.agents/quality-adapter.yaml`.

`docs/artifact-policy.md` and `resolve_artifact_path.py` now tell consumers to resolve `write_artifact_path` before editing quality review artifacts, making symlinked `latest.md` layouts an explicit artifact contract instead of an accidental discovery.

`render_runtime_summary.py` now emits `runtime_visibility_findings`, and a Ceal dogfood run with empty runtime fields surfaces `runtime_visibility_missing_budgets` plus `runtime_visibility_missing_startup_probes` instead of burying the missing timing surface.

The quality core was compressed into a fast-path orchestrator while `references/bootstrap-escalations.md` and `references/inventory-dispatch.md` keep rare bootstrap and inventory detail discoverable from the capability map.

The current `quality` consumer contract remains preserved: existing gates, runtime/test-economics review, delegated-review status, artifact path resolution, and public-skill dogfood remain visible in the core or key references.

Scenario review for this HITL-recommended skill found no need for a new Cautilus artifact while the repo adapter is disabled; deterministic gates, checked-in dogfood, and bounded fresh-eye review own this slice.

The skill-quality and skill-ergonomics references now make validator brittleness explicit: quality review should ask whether exact prose guards prove behavior or merely freeze wording, and should prefer reference-aware or scenario-backed checks when the invariant is not classifier input.

The quality core now names hidden support-skill routing as part of skill review, so support-skill discoverability from workflow language is an explicit quality concern instead of relying on the user to name `support/<id>`.

The quality slow-gate contract now treats pytest temp footprint as a first-class economics signal, including allocated disk bytes, retained pytest sessions, worker fanout, seed totals, and top test temp directories.

`inventory_ci_local_gate_parity.py` now treats `CI-only` quality-gate wording as `ci-only-violation`, and `run-quality` enforces that inventory locally with `--require-empty-parity-issues` instead of leaving the finding advisory-only.

The mutation-testing reference keeps the public `quality` contract runner-neutral while documenting Charness's own Cosmic Ray 8.4.6 dogfood runner as repo-specific support, so consumers can still fill `mutation_testing.commands.*` with their own stack runner.

The quality skill now treats migration-time exact-prose guards as temporary bridges that need an owner and retirement path into structure-aware validators, scenario checks, package-level contracts, or capability artifacts. The current public-spec source-guard inventories report zero rows, while Python exact-phrase contract guards remain a separate migration pressure to review rather than expand.

The mutation-testing workflow now has a dedicated surface obligation covering `.github/workflows/mutation-tests.yml`, `cosmic-ray.toml`, adapter command slots, and exported helper scripts; the reviewed dry-run behavior is pinned so PR dry-runs do not require a full-run Cosmic Ray dump.

The behavior-testing reference keeps agent/user behavior robustness as a `quality` recommendation and artifact-honesty concern while delegating evaluator semantics, execution, and result shape to the Cautilus robustness contract; routine quality review can recommend proof without running live Cautilus.

`recommend_behavior_test.py` emits a structured recommend-only finding with `cautilus.robustness_request.v1`, `cautilus.robustness_plan.v1`, and `cautilus.robustness_report.v1` vocabulary, including relation statuses and expected result fields, so the first #168 slice is executable without a Charness-owned runner.

The agent-production-runtime reference keeps production LLM and agent runtime review inside `quality`: it triggers only from serving-path/runtime evidence, excludes eval fixtures and skill docs by themselves, treats cache economics, overload fallback, idempotency, streaming stalls, and model routing as evidence questions, and reuses existing behavior-proof and external provider proof vocabularies instead of inventing a provider-specific runner.

`validate_skill_ergonomics.py` now emits a structured warning when discoverable skills exist but `skill_ergonomics_gate_rules` is empty, and `run-quality` replays passing phase logs that contain `WARNING`, `WARN`, `WEAK`, or `ADVISORY` lines instead of hiding disabled-enforcement signals behind a green summary.

`validate_skill_ergonomics.py`, its root wrapper, and `validate_adapters.py` now fail on invalid `skill_ergonomics_gate_rules`, so typoed or unknown rule names cannot collapse into an empty-rule advisory pass.

`quality` remains HITL-recommended rather than scenario-owned for this slice; the reviewed consumer contract is explicit in this dogfood case, while deterministic tests prove the adapter-rule failure path.

The quality core now treats `.agents/usage-episodes-adapter.yaml` as a deterministic repo-owned gate: when present, quality resolves and runs the Charness package-root `validate_usage_episodes.py` validator while preserving `no_adapter` and `disabled` as skipped states rather than failures.

`inventory_skill_ergonomics.py` now separates `scope_status` from `finding_status` and preserves `prose_review_status`, so a quality artifact that cites skill ergonomics must distinguish empty scope, zero heuristic findings, and still-required prose review.

Generated quality adapters now default `skill_ergonomics_gate_rules` to the full supported rule set, while an explicit `[]` remains a visible downstream opt-out rather than an accidental disabled default.

`validate_attention_state_visibility.py` now makes exit-zero attention states part of the quality consumer contract: new `no_adapter`, `disabled`, `not_configured`, `skipped`, advisory-only, or prose-review-required helper states must declare warning output, artifact-visible status, support trace, or a local-noop rationale before the standing gate passes.

Scenario review for the Charness mutation-testing workflow kept the public `quality` contract runner-neutral while tightening the dogfood runner around slow-gate economics: sampling now starts from files covered by the declared Cosmic Ray test command, uncovered mutation lines are reported as scope gaps outside the score denominator, partial timeout completion is measured over executable mutants, and per-sampled-file completion blocks misleading `PASS-partial` summaries.

The quality core now treats testability and affected-test selection as structural design questions before tooling questions: `SKILL.md` carries only the language-neutral anchor, while `references/testability-and-selection.md` holds cost/boundary marker guidance, observation-tool tradeoffs, mutation-test implications, and language-specific notes.

Scenario review for #183 kept the public mutation-testing reference language-neutral while making Charness's dogfood runner stricter: coverage contexts derive sampled pytest nodeids, Cosmic Ray work-db probing filters uncovered mutable lines before selection, changed files excluded by sampling block recovery, and `PASS-partial` stays non-closing.

The mutation sampler dogfood now treats configured base/head changed-file diff failure as a fail-closed quality signal: it exits before sample manifests, workflow outputs, or Cosmic Ray config rewrites instead of publishing a fill-only sample from unknown changed-file state.

The affected-test selection contract now says failed selector input discovery is not an empty set: cost-saving selectors should run the broader deterministic gate, while publish/proof selectors should fail with command diagnostics.

The #188 slice_closeout usage-episode repair dogfooded attention-state visibility: the new emitter helper declares `disabled` and `no_adapter` as terminal payload states, and pre-push kept the declaration and file-length contracts blocking instead of letting telemetry no-op behavior read as successful capture.

Standing quality gates that derive proof scope from repo file listing now request strict git-aware listing, so failed `git ls-files` exits with diagnostics instead of falling back to a broad filesystem scan; fixture-friendly helper defaults remain available outside gate-grade proof.

Scenario review for this quality-script slice kept the public `quality` consumer contract unchanged: the representative slow-gate prompt still routes to quality, preserves the artifact/gate/delegated-review expectations, and the semantic change is limited to deterministic standing-test economics inventory tolerating volatile pytest temp cleanup.

cases.prompt: Verify and advance the checked-in release surface without hand-editing generated packaging artifacts.
cases.repo_shape: repo with checked-in version or packaging surfaces and a maintainer-facing release workflow
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `release` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/release/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The public core tells release work to inspect `current_release.py`, `check_real_host_proof.py`, `check_requested_review_gate.py`, git state, and the current release artifact before mutating anything.

The checked-in release core no longer reopens a local-critique loophole: every task-completing release slice records critique, with routine hygiene using a short scoped pass and higher-risk release decisions using the standalone `critique` skill.

The release closeout contract now distinguishes `Critique: short <scope>`, `Critique: full <artifact-or-subagent-status>`, `Critique: not-applicable <reason>`, and `Critique: blocked <host-signal>`, which matches the maintainer-facing path better than implying that a same-agent substitute still counted as release review.
`check_requested_review_gate.py` blocks publish when a configured requested-review command fails or when the release record says review was unavailable without an explicit review waiver.

`publish_release_helpers.backend_command` now enforces a per-op placeholder allowlist at runtime: `release_view` accepts `{tag}`, `release_create` accepts `{tag}` and `{title}`, and `auth_check` accepts none. Adapter templates with unknown placeholders fail fast with the offending placeholder named, mirroring the issue-backend `_resolve_op` hardening so both backend surfaces share the same shape.

`fresh_checkout_probes` is now part of the release adapter contract: `current_release.py` reports whether probes are configured, and `publish_release.py --execute` runs declared probes in a temporary fresh clone after the release commit and before tag push or release creation.

`publish_release.py --execute --close-issue <number>` now preflights `gh issue view` before release mutation, puts close keywords in the direct release commit body, verifies GitHub issue state after push and release creation, records the carrier/manual-fallback evidence, and performs a manual close only if auto-close did not close the issue.

`publish_release.py --execute` now re-reads the public release after `gh release create` and records a post-publish artifact commit when the committed release record needs the verified release URL/status.

Release publish now fails closed when `git diff --name-only <base>..HEAD` cannot compute unreleased paths, before dry-run output or execute-mode version bumps, quality proof, artifacts, commits, tags, pushes, or release creation.
Release publish now also fails closed when configured real-host proof triggers are broken or the proof builder cannot run, while no-trigger repos can still publish without a surfaces manifest.

Release publish now fails closed when previous-tag base-ref lookup or fetch fails, preventing already-pushed release-surface changes from being hidden by an `origin/<branch>` fallback before dry-run output or execute mutation.

Release publish now treats post-create public release verification failure as checked failure instead of pending state: it retries `release view`, writes and pushes a failed-verification recovery artifact, exits nonzero, and skips issue closeout.

`publish_release.py --publish-current` now fails closed when local or remote release tag-history discovery fails while still allowing the explicit no-previous-release-tags case after both discovery commands succeed.

cases.prompt: Use docs/handoff.md as the pickup surface and continue the next highest-leverage workflow from there.
cases.repo_shape: mature repo with an existing handoff artifact and enough adjacent state that the next pickup path can be ambiguous
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `handoff` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `docs/handoff.md` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
reads the current workflow trigger before broad repo exploration and keeps the baton pass continuation-first
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core now makes the `Workflow Trigger` the handoff's primary contract: mention-only pickup should continue the named workflow instead of re-deriving state from summary prose, and the trigger should be explicit enough that the next operator does not have to infer the first move.

That same seam is reinforced by the updated `handoff` references: `workflow-trigger.md` keeps the first move explicit, `state-selection.md` and `spill-targets.md` push stable metrics and proof detail back to their owning artifacts, and always-loaded host instruction surfaces are now non-default in `References`.

That keeps `handoff-workflow-trigger-followup` as a reviewed consumer seam worth re-promoting after the latest chatbot packet churn: pushing the handoff away from history-first writing and back toward continuation-first pickup still maps directly to checked-in `handoff` behavior.

The handoff skill now treats standing invariants and recurring workflow rules as spill/promote candidates: handoff may leave a short pickup pointer, but the durable rule belongs in the owning skill reference, support-runtime contract, spec, validator, or operator document.

cases.prompt: Set up a bounded human review loop for this target so the agent does not auto-decide the final judgment.
cases.repo_shape: repo with a bounded review target and a decision that must stay explicitly human-owned
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `hitl` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/hitl/latest.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps `hitl` narrowly scoped to bounded human review, explicitly preserving what must not be auto-decided and routing state through resumable review artifacts rather than chat-only judgment.

The workflow now requires a Full Target Review after accepted edits are applied or staged: the updated target is presented with an Agent Assessment and Recommended Disposition per `skills/shared/references/agent-assessment-invariant.md`, then `full_target_review` records whole-target acceptance or the need for another pass before the target is closed as accepted.

The workflow now defines an explicit Apply Phase: target file edits happen only after accepted chunks and closing summary, and `require_explicit_apply: true` requires a user apply instruction before touching the target.

The current adapter resolver already pins the visible artifact to `charness-artifacts/hitl/latest.md` and the runtime directory to `.charness/hitl/runtime`, with portable defaults when no adapter is present.

`bootstrap_review.py` now surfaces `require_explicit_apply`, `apply_mode`, and pending `full_target_review` completion metadata in JSON, queue, scratchpad, and state so the apply policy and final whole-target gate are visible at session start.

After a reviewer-requested rewrite is applied to HITL working text or session state, the workflow now requires showing the rewritten chunk excerpt before cursor advancement, asking for accept-or-revise judgment, and recording `applied_rewrite_review_status`.

`bootstrap_review.py` now seeds `applied_rewrite_review_status`, pending rewrite chunk/source fields, queue policy, and scratchpad slots for the applied excerpt, verification, and review result, keeping the applied-rewrite loop resumable without a full runtime engine.

Accepted rules now feed a visible pre-edit constraint gate: before editing or rewriting a chunk, HITL must select active rules, verify target/cursor/queue/bounds alignment, and keep stale or violating chunks active until repaired.

`bootstrap_review.py` now seeds `accepted_rules`, `active_rules_applied`, `target_cursor_checked`, and pre-edit scratchpad slots so a future runtime can record which rules and cursor checks governed each chunk edit.

Before HITL closeout or handoff, `sync_review_artifact.py` now projects hidden runtime state into `charness-artifacts/hitl/latest.md` and `--check` fails when runtime is newer than the durable artifact or target/cursor/queue metadata differs.

The durable HITL checkpoint now includes the runtime session, target, cursor, accepted rules, queue state, next chunk to present, approval boundaries, and links back to hidden runtime files without committing `.charness/hitl/runtime`.

Repo-owned helper scripts make the review loop concrete: `bootstrap_review.py` creates scratchpad, state, queue, rules, and event-log files for a new session, which is current evidence that resumable HITL state is a maintained seam rather than prose-only guidance.

`render_report.py` now turns generated report packets into decision-first HTML cards and structured decisions JSON, with tests proving suggested decisions stay display-only and untouched `unreviewed` cards are dropped.

`render_report.py` now treats adaptive queue metadata as a deterministic decision-ordering contract: explicit stable IDs are required, priority and dependency metadata can reorder source items, and structured review-input effects can reprioritize only the remaining unreviewed queue.
Accepted human answers can now surface a display-only restart recommendation when the remaining queue is stale; superseded items stay explicitly unreviewed rather than becoming approvals.

`report-mode.md` and the public skill core elevate the human-comfort rule: dense tables or generated matrices get plain-language interpretation first, while raw tables stay available as inspectable evidence.

cases.prompt: The concept is still fuzzy; help shape the workflow before we commit to a spec or implementation.
cases.repo_shape: minimal or loosely defined repo context where the request is still concept-shaping rather than implementation-ready
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `ideation` instead of an adjacent public skill
does not invent host-specific adapter state when repo inspection alone should be enough
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps `ideation` upstream of `spec` and `impl`, forcing a living concept model, dependency-ordered questions, and explicit demand/wedge/feedback checks before downstream contract work starts.

The current validation policy already classifies `ideation` as `hitl-recommended` and `adapter-free`, which matches the repo's present stance that concept-shaping quality is better judged through review than through adapter or artifact enforcement.

Deferred decision D10 deliberately preserves a lightweight ideation core with detailed world-model and effectuation playbooks in references, which is the strongest current local evidence that the public trigger stays concise without losing its reasoning boundary.

cases.prompt: Implement the smallest meaningful slice now and verify it against the current repo contract.
cases.repo_shape: repo with an active build slice, existing code or config surfaces, and at least one verification path
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `impl` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/impl` when the skill persists durable state
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core keeps `impl` downstream of `spec` while still requiring an inline honest contract when no separate spec exists, which matches a direct build-slice prompt instead of collapsing into `spec` or `quality`.

The impl stop gate now requires every task-completing repo slice to record critique before closeout, using `Critique: short <scope>` for small local-risk work, `Critique: full <artifact-or-subagent-status>` for broader decisions, and `Critique: not-applicable <reason>` only for inspect/status/routing-only requests.

The impl verification ladder now treats skill self-tests and scheduled or delegated workflows as prescribed-path proof: a passing smoke must start from checked `SKILL.md` commands and selector signals, not an author's free-form external probe.

Repo-owned eval coverage already exercises `impl-adapter-bootstrap`, pinning the inferred adapter defaults for `charness-artifacts/impl` and the verification/truth-surface fields that bootstrap expects.

The impl bootstrap now points at `scripts/plan_risk_interrupt.py`, and the checked-in core tells the agent not to continue plain implementation past a forced debug interrupt until the named spec handoff says that is honest.

The impl core now handles user-corrected agent behavior before mutation by separating stable contracts from better case readings, preserving `no repo change yet` as a valid outcome for judgment-quality corrections, and limiting proof claims to source, wiring, or guidance coverage unless targeted behavior replay or evaluator comparison actually ran.

Scenario review inspected `evals/cautilus/scenarios.json`: `impl` remains mapped to `impl-adapter-bootstrap`, which covers adapter/bootstrap behavior. No registry edit was made because Charness #177 intentionally closes on source and guidance coverage rather than a maintained semantic-quality replay.

cases.prompt: Turn this vague request into a living implementation contract before code changes spread.
cases.repo_shape: repo with an under-specified change request and enough current docs or code to refine a build contract
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `spec` instead of an adjacent public skill
does not invent host-specific adapter state when repo inspection alone should be enough
handles the skill's load-bearing contract without needing the user to restate obvious repo context
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core frames `spec` as turning an under-specified request into the current build contract, which keeps this prompt out of `ideation` and `impl` unless the concept or execution state changes materially.
The spec guardrail now forbids skipping bounded critique before finalizing a task-completing contract, removing the earlier narrow risky/cross-surface qualifier.

Representative contract pins in `scripts/check_skill_contracts.py` now also keep the risk-interrupt carry-forward seam load-bearing: when the planner surfaces a forced debug interrupt, `spec` must consume it explicitly in `Critique` instead of clearing it with generic doc churn.

That keeps the planner-backed `debug -> spec -> impl` boundary visible as a reviewed consumer seam: the current contract makes seam risk survive into the spec handoff before plain implementation is allowed to resume.

cases.prompt: Run a short retro on this slice and persist the repeat trap if the workflow should have caught it.
cases.repo_shape: repo that just completed a meaningful slice or exposed a missed issue that should feed repeat-trap memory
acceptance_evidence: routes the prompt to `retro` instead of an adjacent public skill
names or refreshes `charness-artifacts/retro/recent-lessons.md` when the skill persists durable state
produces an output that a maintainer could review directly without re-deriving the whole request
observed_evidence: The checked-in public core now makes a real workflow miss trigger a short `session` retro before resuming work, and it requires the retro to separate local symptom from the workflow or capability change that would prevent the repeat trap.

That persistence rule is reinforced in `skills/public/retro/references/trigger-and-persistence.md`, and the current repo treats `charness-artifacts/retro/recent-lessons.md` as a first-class memory surface from both `AGENTS.md` and `docs/handoff.md`.

That keeps `retro-structural-cause-followup` as a reviewed consumer seam worth re-promoting after the `v0.4.1` release follow-up work: pushing past a local explanation toward a structural cause and durable memory update is still explicit checked-in `retro` behavior, not only a conversation style preference.

`persist_retro_artifact.py` now normalizes `--artifact-name` to end in `.md` and refuses to overwrite a non-stub `summary_path` when lesson selection yields zero candidates. `--force-empty-summary` is the named opt-in, and `skills/public/retro/SKILL.md` step 5 records the legacy hand-curated summary case so a first retro after legacy adoption does not silently drop curated lessons.

`skills/public/retro/references/phase-aware-efficiency.md` now makes efficiency retros distinguish user-intended exploration from post-triage drift, requiring a triage lock with `fix now`, `deferred`, `needs user call`, and `false positive` before broad exploration can be called waste.

`docs/retro-self-improvement-spec.md` preserves the host boundary from #218: Codex SQLite/TUI cost maps are evidence producers with measured/proxy/unavailable semantics, while the portable `retro` rule owns phase-aware waste interpretation.

`skills/public/retro/scripts/audit_codex_session.py` is now the reviewed Codex evidence-producer path for session efficiency detail: it keeps command snippets off by default, reports token data as `snapshot` or `unavailable` instead of full-session totals, and treats unreadable or schema-drifted sqlite sources as unavailable evidence rather than zero-cost sessions.
